question: Will Israel conduct military action in Greater Beirut on March 22, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Greater Beirut on the listed date Israel Standard Time (IST). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact within Greater Beirut.

For the purposes of this market, “Greater Beirut” refers to the continuous urbanized zone that encompasses all of the Beirut Governorate and the adjacent coastal and suburban municipalities of the Mount Lebanon Governorate. For this market, the geographic boundaries as defined in the map “Location of the Greater Beirut Area” (https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Beirut-3ffb23044f.png) from Faour & Mhawej (2014), Mapping Urban Transitions in the Greater Beirut Area Using Different Space Platforms (https://www.mdpi.com/2073-445X/3/3/941) will be used. If the precise location of a strike cannot be clearly attributed to the defined territory based on the referenced map, it will not qualify.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on the specified territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ground based ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: A US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, effective November 27, 2024, has sharply lowered trader consensus on imminent Israeli military action against Beirut, with markets pricing low probabilities amid de-escalation signals like Israeli troop withdrawals from southern Lebanon and halted cross-border fire. Key drivers include Hezbollah's diminished command structure after Israel's September strikes killing leader Hassan Nasrallah in Beirut's suburbs, alongside diplomatic pressure from the US and France. Recent minor violations persist, but no major Beirut operations reported since late November. Traders eye UNIFIL monitoring and potential Netanyahu government statements, with any verified Hezbollah rocket launches or terror plots risking renewed airstrikes.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact within Greater Beirut.

For the purposes of this market, “Greater Beirut” refers to the continuous urbanized zone that encompasses all of the Beirut Governorate and the adjacent coastal and suburban municipalities of the Mount Lebanon Governorate. For this market, the geographic boundaries as defined in the map “Location of the Greater Beirut Area” (https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Beirut-3ffb23044f.png) from Faour & Mhawej (2014), Mapping Urban Transitions in the Greater Beirut Area Using Different Space Platforms (https://www.mdpi.com/2073-445X/3/3/941) will be used. If the precise location of a strike cannot be clearly attributed to the defined territory based on the referenced map, it will not qualify.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on the specified territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ground based ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: A US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, effective November 27, 2024, has sharply lowered trader consensus on imminent Israeli military action against Beirut, with markets pricing low probabilities amid de-escalation signals like Israeli troop withdrawals from southern Lebanon and halted cross-border fire. Key drivers include Hezbollah's diminished command structure after Israel's September strikes killing leader Hassan Nasrallah in Beirut's suburbs, alongside diplomatic pressure from the US and France. Recent minor violations persist, but no major Beirut operations reported since late November. Traders eye UNIFIL monitoring and potential Netanyahu government statements, with any verified Hezbollah rocket launches or terror plots risking renewed airstrikes.

question: Netanyahu out by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Benjamin Netanyahu ceases to be Prime Minister of Israel for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Benjamin Netanyahu and the government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Netanyahu out by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Benjamin Netanyahu announces that he will resign as Prime Minister of Israel, or otherwise steps down from/is removed from this position by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note that an announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation or removal before the resolution date will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of if/when he actually steps down.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the State of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Netanyahu's tenure as Israeli prime minister reflects his coalition's narrow stability amid Gaza war backlash, Hezbollah escalations, and ongoing corruption trials, with no-confidence motions failing in recent Knesset sessions. Primary drivers include stalled hostage negotiations and public protests eroding support—polls show opposition leaders like Benny Gantz leading hypothetically—but snap elections remain unlikely before 2026 absent major fractures. The ICC's May arrest warrant adds international pressure without domestic ouster mechanism. Upcoming trial testimonies in December and potential Lebanon cease-fires could sway sentiment, as traders weigh his history of political survival against rising dissent.

question: Map 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 3 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 3 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 3, or if Map 3 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: The Last Resort vs HEROIC Academy (BO3) - ESL Challenger League Europe Cup #2 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Lower bracket round 1 match between The Last Resort and HEROIC Academy in the ESL Challenger League Europe Cup #2 Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 18 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "The Last Resort" if The Last Resort win the match against HEROIC Academy.

This market will resolve to "HEROIC Academy" if HEROIC Academy win the match against The Last Resort.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans toward The Last Resort in this BO3 playoff clash with HEROIC Academy at ESL Challenger League Europe Cup #2, driven by their superior recent form, including a semifinal run fueled by strong T-side execution on Mirage and Inferno. HEROIC Academy, despite HEROIC's main roster pedigree, has faltered with inconsistent CT holds and a 1-3 record in recent qualifiers, hampered by academy-level experience gaps. No major roster changes reported; both squads enter rested post-group stage. Key watch: The Last Resort's pistol round dominance (65% win rate) versus Academy's upset potential on Ancient, where they've snagged 40% of maps lately—highlighting volatility in best-of-three formats.

question: Will NRG qualify to Blast Open Rotterdam Playoffs?
description:
The BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 is scheduled to take place from March 18 to March 29, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for the Playoffs of BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026. If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to the Playoffs of BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official BLAST rules.

If the BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 Playoffs participants is not published before April 15, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the BLAST website (https://blast.tv/cs/tournaments). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/counterstrike/BLAST/Open/2026/Spring) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus for playoff qualification at BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 favors teams with strong recent CS2 form from the Perfect World Shanghai Major and BLAST Premier Fall Final, where Team Spirit, G2 Esports, and Vitality posted dominant runs amid roster stability. Recent developments include minor shuffle rumors for underperformers like FaZe Clan, but official lineups remain locked for top contenders, boosting implied probabilities above 70% for favorites. Upcoming Swiss-style group stages emphasize map pool mastery and anti-eco execution, with home-crowd energy in Rotterdam potentially aiding European squads; rest advantages from qualifiers could shift dynamics for jet-lagged invitees, underscoring the wisdom of crowds in pricing volatility around 15-20% upsets.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for the Playoffs of BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026. If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to the Playoffs of BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official BLAST rules.

If the BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 Playoffs participants is not published before April 15, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Vitality's dominant run in recent CS2 tournaments anchors the 56.5% implied probability that no opponent topples them at BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026, reflecting trader consensus on their edge in a high-stakes bracket. The French powerhouse, led by world No. 1 ZywOo and bolstered by flameZ and apEX's veteran leadership, claimed victory at IEM Cologne 2024 and posted top-four finishes across multiple S-tier events this season, showcasing superior map control and clutch execution. Stable roster dynamics and no reported disruptions contrast with rivals like FaZe and G2 facing form dips or key-player inconsistencies, while Vitality's historical BLAST success—unbeaten streaks in prior Opens—bolsters expectations of flawless execution amid Rotterdam's neutral venue. Upsets remain possible given CS2's volatility.

question: Will Iran legalize gay marriage?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the government of Iran legally recognizes marriage between two people of the same sex anywhere within Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Legal recognition refers to any law, constitutional amendment, or binding judicial decision that allows two adults of the same sex to marry with the same legal status as opposite-sex marriages under Iranian law.

Recognition must apply within Iran’s legal system and permit same-sex couples to enter a legally valid marriage recognized by Iranian civil or religious authorities. The recognition must be in force by the listed deadline.

The legalization of civil unions, domestic partnerships, or other forms of limited recognition that do not grant the legal status of marriage will not qualify.

Recognition of marriages performed abroad without permitting such marriages to be performed within Iran will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran's theocratic governance under Sharia law, which criminalizes same-sex relations with severe penalties including execution, drives the 96.3% "No" consensus on legalization of gay marriage, reflecting traders' assessment of entrenched clerical authority led by Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. No recent legislative proposals, reformist gains, or public movements signal change; instead, 2024 reports highlight ongoing LGBTQ crackdowns and executions. This near-unanimous pricing aligns with historical precedents of rigid policy continuity in the Islamic Republic. Realistic shifts would require improbable scenarios like regime collapse amid protests or a hardliner defeat in elections, though Guardian Council vetting limits such outcomes.

question: Will Raja Krishnamoorthi win the Illinois Democratic Senate Primary by 9% or more?
description:
The Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Illinois is scheduled for March 17, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the Democratic Primary election for United States Senator from Illinois.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two candidates receive the same number of valid votes, this market will resolve to the lowest margin of victory bracket for the tied candidate whose last name comes first alphabetically.

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by November 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official election results published by the Illinois State Board of Elections (https://www.elections.il.gov/electionoperations/ElectionResults.aspx); however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors State Sen. Suzy Stratton securing a 6-9% margin of victory over Rep. Raja Krishnamoorthi in the Illinois 14th District Democratic primary, with 97.8% implied probability, driven by consistent internal and public polls showing her lead at 52-46% as of early March 2024. Stratton's commanding position stems from superior fundraising—over $1.2 million raised versus Krishnamoorthi's $800,000—strong county party endorsements, and retail voter outreach in suburban districts. Krishnamoorthi's national profile aids fundraising but trails in ground game. Realistic challenges include a late progressive turnout surge or Stratton gaffe before the March 19 primary, though historical base rates for double-digit polling leads in similar Illinois primaries suggest low upset risk.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for the Grand Final of BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026. If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to the Grand Final of BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official BLAST rules.

If the BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 Finals participants is not published before April 15, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the BLAST website (https://blast.tv/cs/tournaments). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/counterstrike/BLAST/Open/2026/Spring) may also be used.

HLTV world rankings place both in the top three, bolstered by stable rosters featuring stars like ZywOo and donk. question: Will Israel take military action in Gaza on March 20, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Gaza soil on the listed date Israel Standard Time (IST). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "military action" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Gaza ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of the Gaza Strip counts, including buffer zones.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on Gaza territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ground based ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Stalled ceasefire negotiations between Israel and Hamas, coupled with renewed rocket fire from Gaza, are the primary drivers of trader consensus on potential Israeli military action. Recent IDF airstrikes and targeted raids in northern Gaza, including Jabalia, followed intelligence reports of Hamas rebuilding infrastructure, prompting security cabinet approvals for intensified operations. Prime Minister Netanyahu has reiterated no withdrawal without hostage releases, while U.S. diplomatic pressure for restraint adds uncertainty. Traders weigh daily exchanges against upcoming Qatar-mediated talks, which could shift probabilities if breakthroughs occur or collapse. Historical patterns show such escalations often follow lulls in diplomacy.

eventMetadata.context_description: A US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, effective November 27, 2024, has driven down trader consensus on near-term Israeli military action against Lebanon, with markets reflecting low implied probabilities amid fragile de-escalation. Key factors include mutual accusations of violations—such as Hezbollah rocket fire and Israeli airstrikes—but no major ground incursions since Israel's October limited operation in southern Lebanon. Diplomatic pressure from the US and Qatar, coupled with UNIFIL monitoring reinforcements, supports restraint, though Netanyahu's vows to counter Hezbollah rebuilding keep risks alive. Traders eye December compliance reports and potential Houthi spillover as pivotal catalysts.

question: Saudi Arabia military action against Yemen by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Saudi Arabia initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Yemeni soil or any official Yemen embassy or consulate by the listed date, 11:59 PM Arabian Standard Time (GMT+3). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "military action" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Saudi Arabia's military forces that impact Yemen ground territory or any official Yemen embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Yemen soil is hit by an Saudi Arabian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on Yemen territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Saudi Arabian ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If no qualifying strike is confirmed by the resolution date, this market will remain open until the end of the second day after the resolution time. If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by that time, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: The sustained truce between Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthis, in place since April 2022 and repeatedly extended, forms the primary driver behind traders' 89% implied probability favoring no Saudi military action by March 31. Official Saudi statements emphasize diplomatic resolution over escalation, aligning with Vision 2030 economic diversification goals that prioritize stability. Recent developments, including January reports of Saudi concessions in peace talks mediated by Oman and tacitly supported by post-China deal ties with Iran, reinforce de-escalation despite Houthi Red Sea disruptions. No announcements of troop mobilizations or airstrikes have emerged, underscoring trader consensus on continued restraint amid regional volatility.

question: Houthi military action against Saudi Arabia by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Houthis initiate a drone, missile, or air strike that impacts Saudi Arabia's
ground territory between market creation and the listed date, 11:59 PM Arabian Standard Time (GMT+3). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Houthi forces that physically impact land under Saudi Arabia's control.

Saudi Arabia's ground territory, for the purposes of this market, refers to land internationally recognized as part of the State of Saudi Arabia as of market creation. 

Missiles or drones that are intercepted before reaching the ground, or surface-to-air missile strikes, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether debris lands on Saudi Arabia's soil or causes damage.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: The sustained UN-brokered truce between Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthis, in place since April 2022 and repeatedly extended, forms the primary driver behind traders' 87.5% implied probability favoring no Houthi military action against Saudi Arabia by March 31. Saudi-led coalition forces have largely withdrawn from offensive operations in Yemen, prioritizing diplomatic negotiations mediated by Oman and the UN, with recent rounds in 2024 yielding progress on economic de-escalation. Houthis have redirected drone and missile strikes toward Red Sea shipping amid the Israel-Gaza conflict, with no verified attacks on Saudi territory since sporadic 2022 incidents. Absent provocations or breakdowns in talks, this low-risk environment underpins the market's consensus.

question: Blast Open Rotterdam 2026: Will a player break something during a game?
description:
BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 is scheduled to take place from March 18 to March 29, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if any player participating in an official match at BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 visibly breaks equipment during or immediately following that match by March 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

“Breaks equipment” means a player’s actions visibly damage, crack, snap, shatter, or otherwise render an item unusable or clearly broken. Examples of qualifying items include a mouse, keyboard, monitor, desk, or chair. Mere striking, slamming, or knocking over equipment without visible breakage will not qualify.

Only actions taken by a player during live match play, or immediately after the conclusion of that player’s official match while still at the match setup or on the official broadcast, will count. Damage occurring before a match, outside the official match area, during practice, backstage, or away from the broadcasted match environment will not qualify.

This market concerns only players participating in official BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 matches. Actions by coaches, staff, referees, audience members, or other non-players will not qualify.

If BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if no official match of the tournament is completed by April 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source will be the official BLAST broadcast, including the BLAST livestream on Twitch  (https://www.twitch.tv/blastpremier). Only video from the official Blast broadcast/stream will qualify.

The resolution source will be the official BLAST broadcast, including the BLAST livestream on Twitch  (https://www.twitch.tv/blastpremier). Only video from the official Blast broadcast/stream will qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans heavily against a player breaking equipment or gear during the Blast Open Rotterdam 2026 matches, with "No" implying 76% probability, reflecting the rarity of such incidents in professional Counter-Strike 2 LAN events. Historically, BLAST tournaments feature disciplined pros under high pressure, where tilt-induced destruction—like smashing keyboards or monitors—occurs infrequently, with no confirmed cases in recent majors such as BLAST Fall Final 2024 or Paris Major. Recent form shows top teams like Vitality and FaZe maintaining composure amid intense playoffs, bolstered by team psychologists and event rules minimizing disruptions. While upsets and momentum shifts are common, the controlled Rotterdam stage and future scheduling favor focus over frustration, aligning with crowd wisdom on low-risk outbursts.

question: Will China invade Taiwan by September 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of the Republic of China (Taiwan) by September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Territory under the administration of the Republic of China, including any inhabited islands, will qualify; however, uninhabited islands will not qualify. 

The resolution source for this market will be official confirmation by China, Taiwan, the United Nations, or any permanent member of the UN Security Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 92.5% implied probability of no Chinese invasion of Taiwan by September 30, 2026, driven primarily by the absence of concrete military indicators for an amphibious assault, such as large-scale troop mobilizations or logistics buildup, despite ongoing People's Liberation Army exercises in the Taiwan Strait. Recent October 2024 PLA drills responded to President Lai Ching-te's National Day speech but mirrored prior "gray-zone" patterns without escalation signals. Beijing's rhetoric under Xi Jinping prioritizes "reunification" while avoiding firm timelines, amid economic pressures and U.S. deterrence via arms sales and alliances like AUKUS. Analysts cite PLA modernization targets for 2027 as a base rate suggesting unreadiness, with traders pricing in high costs of conflict.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 76.5% implied probability against a Chinese invasion of Taiwan by December 31, 2027, driven primarily by robust U.S. deterrence through arms sales, Taiwan Relations Act commitments, and alliances like AUKUS, which raise prohibitive military and economic costs for Beijing. Recent PLA exercises around the Taiwan Strait, including large-scale drills in May and October 2024 following President Lai Ching-te's inauguration and UN speech, signal pressure tactics rather than invasion preparations, as confirmed by U.S. intelligence assessments deeming an attack imminent unlikely. China's economic headwinds, including property sector woes and sluggish growth, further dampen aggressive adventurism, while Xi Jinping's public emphasis on "peaceful reunification" and 2027 PLA modernization goals prioritizes capability over conflict. Upcoming U.S. elections and cross-strait dialogues add uncertainty to this low-risk trader view.

question: Israeli forces cross the Litani River by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Israeli military personnel cross the Litani River in Lebanon by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Israeli military personnel” refers to members of the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) or any other official military units acting under the authority of the State of Israel. Intelligence or other non-military personnel will not count.

A “crossing” will be considered to have occurred if Israeli military personnel are confirmed to have physically traversed the Litani River in Lebanon at any point, including but not limited to by bridge, boat, swimming, wading, or temporary or permanent crossing.

Aerial insertion, parachute drops, helicopter landings, or other forms of aerial infiltration that do not involve Israeli military personnel physically traversing the Litani River will not qualify.

Mere presence on one bank of the Litani River, without confirmation that Israeli military personnel traversed the river to the opposite bank, will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus tilts slightly toward "No" at 51.5% implied probability for Israeli forces crossing the Litani River by June 30, driven by the absence of confirmed IDF advances beyond southern Lebanon positions up to 3 km from the border, despite intensified airstrikes and ground raids against Hezbollah targets. This competitive balance stems from Israel's stated aim for a security buffer zone north of the river—echoed in Netanyahu's recent comments—clashing with ongoing U.S.-brokered ceasefire talks and Hezbollah's resilient defenses amid reported leadership losses. Escalatory developments like a major ground push or diplomatic breakthrough could swiftly shift odds, while the compressed timeline adds uncertainty to market pricing.

question: Will Iran take military action against a Gulf State on March 21, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on a Gulf State's soil on the listed date in Arabia Time (GMT+3). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Qualifying "Gulf States" are as follows: Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "military action" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a gulf state's ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of a listed gulf state counts.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on a gulf state's territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Iranian ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary solution resolution source will be official government/military statements (Iranian or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for direct Iranian military action against Gulf states like Saudi Arabia or UAE, driven by mutual economic deterrence and diplomatic progress. Recent Israel-Iran missile exchanges in October 2024 escalated regional tensions, prompting Iranian warnings to Gulf hosts of U.S. and Israeli forces against aiding strikes, yet Riyadh and Abu Dhabi have urged restraint and deepened ties via the 2023 China-brokered détente. U.S. naval deployments in the Persian Gulf bolster defenses, while Houthi disruptions indirectly pressure shipping lanes without prompting overt retaliation. Traders eye upcoming IAEA nuclear talks and potential Israeli reprisals as key catalysts for volatility.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on a Gulf State's soil on the listed date in Arabia Time (GMT+3). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Qualifying "Gulf States" are as follows: Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "military action" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a gulf state's ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of a listed gulf state counts.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on a gulf state's territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Iranian ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary solution resolution source will be official government/military statements (Iranian or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for direct Iranian military action against Gulf states like Saudi Arabia or UAE, driven by mutual economic deterrence and diplomatic progress. Recent Israel-Iran missile exchanges in October 2024 escalated regional tensions, prompting Iranian warnings to Gulf hosts of U.S. and Israeli forces against aiding strikes, yet Riyadh and Abu Dhabi have urged restraint and deepened ties via the 2023 China-brokered détente. U.S. naval deployments in the Persian Gulf bolster defenses, while Houthi disruptions indirectly pressure shipping lanes without prompting overt retaliation. Traders eye upcoming IAEA nuclear talks and potential Israeli reprisals as key catalysts for volatility.

question: Pakistan military action against Kabul by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Pakistan initiates a drone, missile, or air strike within the municipality of Kabul by the specified date Afghanistan Time (GMT+4:30). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "military action" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Pakistani military forces that impact within one of the 22 municipal districts of Kabul.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land within the municipality of Kabul or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ground based ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Pakistani ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tensions between Pakistan and Afghanistan persist over cross-border militant attacks by Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) fighters allegedly sheltered in Afghan territory, but traders price low odds of Pakistani military action targeting Kabul by March 31 due to restrained official rhetoric and active diplomatic channels. Recent Pakistani airstrikes in December 2024 hit TTP camps in Afghanistan's border regions without advancing toward the capital, while Taliban spokesmen rejected invasion threats and urged talks. Islamabad's demands focus on extraditions rather than escalation, and no troop mobilizations or ultimatums signal imminent operations. This de-escalatory posture, amid economic pressures on both sides, underpins the 61% "No" consensus reflecting traders' assessment of continued restraint.

question: Pakistan military action against Afghanistan by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Pakistan initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on the soil of Afghanistan or any official Afghan embassy or consulate by the listed date, 11:59 PM Afghanistan Time (GMT+4:30). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "military action" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Pakistani military forces that impact Afghan ground territory or any official Afghan embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Afghan soil is hit by a Pakistani missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on Afghan territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ground based ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Pakistani ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Pakistan initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on the soil of Afghanistan or any official Afghan embassy or consulate by the listed date, 11:59 PM Afghanistan Time (GMT+4:30). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "military action" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Pakistani military forces that impact Afghan ground territory or any official Afghan embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Afghan soil is hit by a Pakistani missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Pakistani airstrikes into Afghanistan targeting Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) militants in late December 2024, following attacks that killed over a dozen soldiers, form the core driver of the 64.5% implied probability for military action by March 31. Afghan Taliban officials condemned the strikes as sovereignty violations, expelled Pakistani diplomats, and threatened retaliation amid ongoing border clashes and refugee deportations. Pakistan's military reiterated its right to self-defense against TTP safe havens, with no diplomatic breakthroughs despite talks. Trader consensus reflects persistent cross-border militancy, historical tit-for-tat escalations, and upcoming TTP threats as key risks elevating odds.

question: Will "War Machine" be the top global Netflix movie this week?
description:
Netflix is expected to update its global Top 10 TV movies list on top10.netflix.com on Tuesday, March 24, 2026, 3:00 PM ET, reflecting viewership from the previous week (Monday to Sunday).

This market will resolve based on which movie this update ranks as the #1 global Netflix movie.

The ranking is based on total views globally, as reported by Netflix for Global Top 10 Movies (English only).

If the top10.netflix.com update does not occur by March 27, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man holds a commanding 94.5% implied probability as this week's top global Netflix movie, propelled by its blockbuster debut with over 50 million hours viewed in the first weekend, per official Netflix metrics, leveraging the iconic series' loyal fanbase and strong critical acclaim for Cillian Murphy's return. Trader consensus aligns with historical IP dominance, where franchise extensions like this crush competitors amid light new-release slate. War Machine lingers at 5.5% on residual momentum, but upsets hinge on rare data revisions or viral surges for underdogs like Nobody 2, though chart lock-in typically solidifies by midweek updates.

This market will resolve based on which movie this update ranks as the #1 Netflix movie in the United States.

The ranking is based on total views in the United States, as reported by Netflix for movies.

If the top10.netflix.com update does not occur by March 27, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly backs Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man as the top US Netflix movie this week at 83% implied probability, propelled by its blockbuster debut and dominant position on Netflix's latest Top 10 English films chart, where it racked up the most hours viewed. The film's high-profile tie to the iconic series has sparked viral fan engagement and critical buzz, solidifying its lead over distant challengers like War Machine (3.8%), buoyed by Brad Pitt nostalgia but fading fast. Nobody 2's 2.5% odds stem from modest premiere traction, while niche entries like Kpop Demon Hunters lag due to limited mainstream appeal. Tuesday's chart refresh will lock in the resolution based on streaming metrics.

question: Will "Hoppers" 3rd Weekend Box Office be less than 16.5m?
description:
This market will resolve according to how much "Hoppers" Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its 3rd weekend. The "Daily" figures found on the “Domestic Daily” tab on this movie's Box Office Mojo (https://www.boxofficemojo.com/title/tt28650488/) will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day weekend (March 20 - March 22) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the BoxOfficeMojo figures provided under Domestic Daily performance for the 3-day weekend, regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is no final data available by March 29, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

events.title: "Hoppers" 3rd Weekend Box Office
events.description:
This market will resolve according to how much "Hoppers" Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its 3rd weekend. The "Daily" figures found on the “Domestic Daily” tab on this movie's Box Office Mojo (https://www.boxofficemojo.com/title/tt28650488/) will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day weekend (March 20 - March 22) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the BoxOfficeMojo figures provided under Domestic Daily performance for the 3-day weekend, regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is ambiguity as to whether the resolution source's figures are final, this market will remain open until both https://www.boxofficemojo.com/ and https://www.the-numbers.com/ have confirmed their finalized figures.

If there is no final data available by March 29, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pins "Hoppers'" third weekend box office at $16.5-18 million (80% implied probability), propelled by early Friday estimates showing a healthy 38% drop from last weekend's $28.2 million, fueled by strong family turnout and an A- CinemaScore. Positive word-of-mouth on platforms like X underscores sustained interest in the animated adventure, despite competition from "Red One" holdovers and new wide releases. Historical patterns for mid-budget animations suggest 35-45% weekly declines with good reviews, aligning with the 18-19.5m tranche at 16%. Minimal tail risks reflect robust legs, with final Sunday figures key amid pre-Thanksgiving positioning.

The "Daily" figures found on the “Domestic Daily” tab on this movie's Box Office Mojo (https://www.boxofficemojo.com/title/tt28650488/) will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day weekend (March 20 - March 22) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the BoxOfficeMojo figures provided under Domestic Daily performance for the 3-day weekend, regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is no final data available by March 29, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pins "Hoppers'" third weekend box office at $16.5-18 million (80% implied probability), propelled by early Friday estimates showing a healthy 38% drop from last weekend's $28.2 million, fueled by strong family turnout and an A- CinemaScore. Positive word-of-mouth on platforms like X underscores sustained interest in the animated adventure, despite competition from "Red One" holdovers and new wide releases. Historical patterns for mid-budget animations suggest 35-45% weekly declines with good reviews, aligning with the 18-19.5m tranche at 16%. Minimal tail risks reflect robust legs, with final Sunday figures key amid pre-Thanksgiving positioning.

question: Kylian Mbappe: Anytime Goalscorer
description: In the upcoming La Liga game between Real Madrid CF and Club Atlético de Madrid, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 4:00 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kylian Mbappe is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". Own goals do not count. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "50-50". If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Real Madrid CF vs. Club Atlético de Madrid - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the La Liga game between Real Madrid CF and Club Atlético de Madrid, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 4:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Griezmann's confirmed muscle injury sidelines Atletico's talismanic playmaker for the September 29 Madrid Derby at Wanda Metropolitano, tilting trader consensus toward elevated props for Real Madrid attackers like Mbappé, who's netted four La Liga goals early this season amid a clinical streak, and Vinícius Júnior, thriving with three goals and four assists. Real's bolstered defense—Courtois fit, Militão recovered—caps Atletico counters from Julián Álvarez or Morata, whose form lags. Simeone's compact setup historically stifles derbies (under 2.5 goals in four of last five), tempering shot/on-target overs, while Ancelotti's rest edge post-midweek favors Madrid's high press, pressuring Atlético's depleted midfield. Odds reflect crowd wisdom on Real's 55% implied win probability.

question: Will the highest temperature in London be 13°C on March 22?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 22 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ca/mississauga/CYYZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors a high of 12°C or below in Toronto on March 22 (99.9% implied probability), driven by Environment Canada's forecast of just 3°C amid cloudy skies and 30% chance of flurries, corroborated by Weather Network and AccuWeather models showing persistent cold air mass from a late-winter synoptic pattern. Historical March 22 averages hover around 7°C, with records rarely exceeding 20°C, reinforcing this positioning as ensemble weather models have converged on subdued highs. Realistic challenges include an unforeseen warm front or significant forecast bust, though low-confidence outliers price such upsets at under 0.2% across higher bins, aligning with model reliability for short-range predictions.
